# Path behaviors from a subdirectory of the sesam repo (like git):
#   #1 `add ./local` from sub/ registers the secret as the repo-relative
#      `sub/local` (the API/config never store cwd-relative paths).
#   #2 `status` renders paths relative to the cwd: `local` from sub/, but
#      `sub/local` from the repo root.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

# Init at the worktree root: the sesam dir is the repo root itself.
exec sesam init --user admin

mkdir sub
cp secretfile sub/local

# #1: add from within sub/ using a cwd-relative path.
cd sub
exec sesam add ./local

# The config records the repo-relative path, not the cwd-relative `local`.
cd $WORK
grep 'sub/local' sesam.yml

# --json is the stable machine interface and always reports repo-relative paths.
exec sesam status --json
stdout '"sub/local"'

# #2: from sub/, the tree shows the path relative to the cwd (`local`); the
# parent `sub` node does not appear. --all so the freshly sealed (in-sync)
# secret is listed.
cd sub
exec sesam status --all
stdout 'local'
! stdout 'sub'

# From the repo root the same secret shows under its full `sub` tree node.
cd $WORK
exec sesam status --all
stdout 'sub'
stdout 'local'

-- secretfile --
top secret
